title: Module Functor
code:
module type Printable = sig
  type t
  val to_string : t -> string
end

module MakeLogger (P : Printable) = struct
  let log prefix value =
    Printf.printf "[%s] %s\n" prefix (P.to_string value)

  let log_list prefix values =
    List.iter (log prefix) values
end

module IntLogger = MakeLogger (struct
  type t = int
  let to_string = string_of_int
end)

let () = IntLogger.log_list "INFO" [1; 2; 3]
description: Demonstrates OCaml's module functors — parameterized modules that take other modules as arguments. `MakeLogger` accepts any module satisfying `Printable` and produces a specialized logger. A powerful alternative to typeclasses for abstraction.
